question.text: Right of First Refusal (ROFR) in a PE investment gives the AIF:

answer.text: The right to be the first to receive dividends
The right to match any offer received by the promoter for their shares before a sale to a third party
The right to appoint the company's auditor
The right to exit the investment at any time

explanation.text: **The Logic:** ROFR gives the AIF the right to match any bona fide offer received by the promoter before the promoter can sell to a third party. If the AIF matches the offer, it can purchase the shares instead of the third-party buyer. **The Trap:** ROFR is triggered only when the promoter wants to sell. It's a defensive right — the AIF doesn't have to buy; it has the option to match if it wants to prevent an unwanted third party from entering. **Cross-Exam Context:** Transfer restrictions (ROFR, tag along, drag along) are tested in XIX-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ROFR protects AIF investors from undesirable changes in ownership. Combined with tag along rights, it ensures the AIF can either maintain its co-investor relationship or exit on favorable terms.
